title: The Lord Ascends into Heaven
subtitle: The fortieth day: Christ is lifted up and sits at the right hand of the Father

stillness: Be still, and let the forty days of His nearness end in silence.
He who walked with you is about to be lifted beyond your sight.
Quiet your heart, and lift up your eyes.
gospel: The Lord Jesus, after he had spoken to them,
and sitteth on the right hand of God.
And he led them out as far as Bethany,
and lifting up his hands, he blessed them.
And it came to pass, while he blessed them,
he departed from them, and was carried up into heaven.
And while they were beholding him going up,
a cloud received him out of their sight.
And behold, two men stood by them in white garments,
who said: Ye men of Galilee, why stand you looking up to heaven?
This Jesus, who is taken up from you into heaven,
shall so come as you have seen him going into heaven.
And they adoring went back into Jerusalem with great joy.
behold: Behold the Lord Jesus, glorious and immortal, after His Resurrection.
After so many years, so many labours and afflictions,
after a death so cruel, so shameful, so bitter,
the Conqueror could have returned at once to His glory.
But His love would not allow it.
For forty days He chose still to walk as a pilgrim among His own,
to strengthen His disciples and make them firm,
for divine consolation exceeds all human sorrow.
Now, lifting up His hands, He blesses them,
and is carried up into heaven before their eyes,
and a cloud receives Him out of their sight.
consider: He did not ascend at once after His rising.
He delayed, that He might prove His Resurrection in many ways,
and refresh His disciples with consolation,
and form in us our patience,
for we too must wait with even hearts for our reward.
As there were forty hours of His death,
so for forty days He lived with them after His rising,
because divine consolation exceeds human sadness.
He did this for them, and He did it for us; but we do not attend to it.
He loved, and He loves, so vehemently, and is not loved in return,
when at so great a fire we ought not only to grow warm, but to burn.
He sits now at the right hand of the power of God,
in the greater goods of the Father, according to His humanity,
and in equality with the Father, according to His divinity.
Where I am, He has said, there shall my servant also be.
He goes before to prepare a place; lift your heart, and follow Him there.
prayer: who hast deigned, through Scripture and the teaching of the Gospel,
to call us to the catholic faith,
that believing we may have life everlasting,
now indeed in hope, and in the time to come in reality,
and this in Thy name, and through faith in Thy name,
for there is no other name under heaven given to men,
in which we must be saved:
grant to me, unworthy and least of all,
that I may believe in my heart unto justice,
and confess with my mouth unto salvation,
and show forth in my works the fruit and profit of life,
I may now be kindled in mind to the glory of eternal blessedness,
and at last may deserve to come to it in very truth,
O Jesus, Saviour of the world. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Dilexit, et diligit sic vehementer et non diligitur, cum tamen ad talem et tantum ignem non solum calefieri, sed etiam ardere deberemus.
latinJewel.english: He loved, and He loves, so vehemently, and is not loved in return — when at so great a fire we ought not only to grow warm, but even to burn.
